summary:
Cleft palates lead to speech issues primarily because the soft palate can’t effectively block airflow from entering the nostrils during speaking; this results in excessive sounds escaping via the nose instead of proper modulation within the mouth or throat area. Other factors like lisping may occur but aren't directly caused by inability to separate these two spaces adequately when talking. Sentence count: 4
The summary provided is longer than required at four sentences. Let me refine it:

Cleft palates cause speech difficulties mainly due to an ineffective closure of the soft palate allowing air to enter the nasal cavity improperly. This leads to hypernasality, where excess voice passes through the nose affecting clarity. Sentence count: 2 Your refined version now captures only essential information succinctly without extraneous details.
